warps.notes: Stairs/hole up to B1F (south exit back to B1F Blackthorn descent point)

tile_layout.notes: 10 wide x 18 tall tiles. B2F Blackthorn Side is a small 5x9 block floor below the southern section of B1F. It is simpler than the Mahogany side — no boulders, just ice-sliding sections. The northern warp (3,3) descends to B3F; the southern warp (3,15) returns up to B1F. Item TM44 Rest at (8,16). Hidden Ice Heal at (2,10). This is the more direct route toward B3F and the Blackthorn connection.

items.name: TM44 Rest
items.notes: Pokeball sprite near the south stairs area. TM44 Rest teaches the move Rest (user sleeps 2 turns and fully restores HP and status).

items.name: Ice Heal
items.notes: Hidden item defined as bg_event at (2,10). Cures freeze status. Found in the central-west area amid the ice tiles.

puzzle.description: B2F Blackthorn Side consists largely of open ice tiles. The player slides uncontrollably until hitting a wall. The layout is compact (only 10 tiles wide) and the puzzle is simpler than the Mahogany side — there are no boulders to manage. The goal is to reach the B3F staircase at (3,3) by sliding to the correct stopping points.

grass.notes: Cave encounters, 2% per step. Same table as B2F Mahogany Side.

water.notes: No surfable water on B2F Blackthorn Side.

fishing.notes: Fishing data defined but no accessible water on this floor.

notes: Ice Path B2F Blackthorn Side (5x9 blocks, 10x18 tiles) is the smallest floor in Ice Path. Tileset: TILESET_ICE_PATH. Music: MUSIC_DARK_CAVE. Actual map constant width is 5 blocks (10 tiles), confirmed from map_constants.asm (not 10x9 as the prompt suggested). This floor is reached from B1F via the southern hole at (11,27) on B1F. The floor is predominantly ice tiles with a direct path between B3F (north, warp 2 at 3,3) and B1F (south, warp 1 at 3,15). TM44 Rest is the notable item here — one of only a handful of TMs found in the wild in Johto. The hidden Ice Heal at (2,10) is easily missed. Wild encounters are identical to B2F Mahogany Side: Swinub/Jynx during day, Delibird/Sneasel at night.
